req.title: Joint account operations (boundary variant)
req.statement: A joint-account holder shall perform transactions; the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.
error_pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change

telemetry.raw_response_head:
# Pytest Tests: Joint Account Holder Transactions & Ownership Modification

```python
"""
Test Suite: Joint Account Holder Transactions & Ownership Restrictions
Requirement: A joint-account holder shall perform transactions;
             the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership.
             Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Optional
from enum import Enum, auto


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain model (production code assumed to live in src/banking/)
# Importing here; replace paths with your actual module structure
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
try:
    from src.banking.account import JointAccount, AccountHolder, HolderRole
    from src.banking.transaction import Transaction, TransactionType
    from src.banking.exceptions import (
        InsufficientFundsError,
        UnauthorisedOwnershipChangeError,
        TransactionLimitExceededError,
        BelowMinimumBalanceError,
        InvalidTransactionAmountError,
    )
except ImportError:
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Inline stub implementation so the test file is self-contained and
    # runnable while the real production code is being developed.
    # Remove this block once production code exists.
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    from decimal import Decimal
    from enum import Enum, auto
    from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    from typing import List, Optional

    class HolderRole(Enum):
        PRIMARY = auto()
        SECONDARY = auto()

    class TransactionType(Enum):
        DEPOSIT = auto()
        WITHDRAWAL = auto()
        TRANSFER = auto()

    class InsufficientFundsError(Exception):
        pass

    class UnauthorisedOwnershipChangeError(PermissionError):
        pass

    class TransactionLimitExceededError(Exception):
        pass

    class BelowMinimumBalanceError(Exception):
        pass

    class InvalidTransactionAmountError(ValueError):
        pass

    @dataclass
    class AccountHolder:
        holder_id: str
        name: str
        role: HolderRole

    @dataclass
    class Transaction:
        transaction_type: TransactionType
        amount: Decimal
        initiated_by: "AccountHolder"

    @dataclass
    class JointAccount:
        account_id: str
        primary_holder: AccountHolder
        secondary_holder: AccountHolder
        balance: Decimal = Decimal("0.00")
        minimum_balance: Decimal = Decimal("0.00")
        max_single_transaction: Decimal = Decimal("10000.00")
        max_daily_limit: Decimal = Decimal("20000.00")
        _daily_total: Decimal = field(default=Decimal("0.00"), init=False, repr=False)
        _transaction_history: List[Transaction] = field(
            default_factory=list, init=False, repr=False
        )

        # ---- helpers -------------------------------------------------------
        def _is_holder(self, holder: AccountHolder) -> bool:
            return holder.holder_id in (
                self.primary_holder.holder_id,
                self.secondary_holder.holder_id,
            )

        def _validate_holder(self, holder: AccountHolder) -> None:
            if not self._is_holder(holder):
                raise PermissionError(f"{holder.holder_id} is not a holder on this account.")

        def _validate_amount(self, amount: Decimal) -> None:
            if amount <= Decimal("0"):
                raise InvalidTransactionAmountError("Amount must be positive.")
            if amount > self.max_single_transaction:
                raise TransactionLimitExceededError(
                    f"Amount {amount} exceeds single-transaction limit {self.max_single_transaction}."
                )
